role: President of Argentina (2007–2015); accused by prosecutor Alberto Nisman of treason for the 2013 MOU with Iran
bio: Cristina Fernández de Kirchner served as President of Argentina from 2007 to 2015, succeeding her husband Néstor Kirchner. During her second term, her government signed the [Iran–Argentina MOU](/events/iran-argentina-mou-2013) on 27 January 2013 — a memorandum of understanding with the Iranian government that established a joint truth commission to reinvestigate the [AMIA bombing](/events/amia-bombing-1994) of 1994. In January 2015, AMIA special prosecutor [Alberto Nisman](/people/alberto-nisman) filed a 289-page charging document accusing Kirchner and Foreign Minister [Jorge Taiana](/people/jorge-taiana) of treason and cover-up, alleging that the MOU was designed to shield Iranian suspects from Interpol red notices and Argentine prosecution in exchange for oil and trade concessions. On 18 January 2015 — the night before Nisman was scheduled to testify before Congress — he was found dead in his apartment. His death, initially ruled a suicide, was determined to be homicide by a federal court in 2019. The investigation into who ordered the killing and the validity of the treason charges against Kirchner remain contested and politically charged issues in Argentina.

sources.title: Argentine prosecutor Alberto Nisman found dead

editorial_notes: Nisman filed charges against her and Taiana on January 14 2015. She dismissed the case as politically motivated. 2019 federal court found Nisman murdered; chain of responsibility still investigated.
